title: 2025 01 21 Huck Get Less Fat Plan 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • 10-week joint accountability program Yahir wrote for huck reed — Jan 15 to Mar 26, 2025, with March 26 = Results Day.
  • Five non-negotiables: no food after 8pm (3hr before bed), 4 runs/week minimum, 1 strength session/week (legs-focused), every-other-day check-ins, water > alcohol.
  • Tech stack: strava for runs, Apple Health for tracking, iMessage thread for daily 👍/👎 check-ins. Sunday night = weekly reset call.
  • Includes a full half-marathon-style training table by week — Phase 1 (Get Started) → Phase 2 (Leveling Up) → Phase 3 (Full Send) — with named zones (Z1 conversational → Z5 max effort) and prescribed mileage per day.
  • Tone: "friendly discipline," "Perfect is boring — we're aiming for consistent and sustainable," "Drop a 🚀 in our iMessage thread when ready" — Yahir as coach-friend, not drill sergeant.

Notes

This is the same template Yahir keeps deploying for friends (compare to the PT playbook for Austin clients later in the year): shared metric, lightweight check-in, weekly reset, named results day. The format is reusable; the content is specific to each person's body and schedule.

Huck is positioned as the latent runner — "you've done the running thing before (4x a week was solid!)" — so the plan asks for re-activation, not new acquisition. The Sun-Mar-26 finish-line date isn't tied to a race; it's pure deadline psychology, the 10 week activation arc.

Notable: alcohol policy is "not saying don't drink, but reduce on average" + the explicit "no drunk food after cutoff" rule. The doc ends with intel-gathering questions (best check-in time, hype music) — Yahir acquiring the parameters needed to keep the loop sticky.

This is one of Yahir's recurring templates of investing in friends' bodies — appears across brotein context and the later Conrad/Hector PT relationships.

Connections

  • huck reed — the recipient
  • strava — accountability surface
  • 60 day activation rule — the 10-week container
  • brotein — the broader fitness-with-friends thesis
  • 2025 11 04 personal training approach — the same template, now monetized
